Scenario.title: Spearphishing Attack Leading to System Disruption in Transportation Sector
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple This scenario depicts a straightforward cyber attack campaign where threat actors use spearphishing techniques to gain initial access, execute commands indirectly, and disrupt operations by manipulating network devices.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disrupt operational integrity or steal sensitive data through targeted deception.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Operational Workstations, Network Devices
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Social Engineering, Malicious Code, Exploitation of Vulnerabilities

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2025-27363 (Suggested): Out-of-Bounds Write Vulnerability in Font Parsing
stage.description: The attackers employ spearphishing emails that closely resemble official correspondence from trusted entities within the transportation industry, such as shipping companies or logistics providers. These emails contain links designed to lure recipients into clicking, thereby exploiting vulnerabilities in internet-facing applications or systems to establish unauthorized entry points.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2017-1000253: Stack Buffer Corruption in Load_Elf_Binary Function
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Once initial access is achieved through the spearphishing link, the threat actors utilize compromised credentials or system vulnerabilities to run indirect command executions. This involves leveraging flaws in software components, such as command-line interfaces on workstations managing scheduling or monitoring tasks, allowing them to execute arbitrary code without direct interaction, thus automating further actions within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: In this final stage, the attackers manipulate the response content from affected systems or exploits to alter configurations on network devices. By bridging between trusted and untrusted segments or injecting false data, they disrupt transportation services, causing potential delays or failures in real-time operations, thereby achieving their desired impact through subtle changes in device behavior or settings.

Scenario.summary: A simple expert-level attack campaign begins with targeted spearphishing to breach defenses, proceeds with automated command execution on vulnerable workstations, and culminates in operational disruptions by compromising network boundaries.
